top_gaps_json: [{"id": "PRD-030", "severity": "important", "label": "Validate catalog mapping into canonical shows", "why": "This matters because weak validation at the catalog boundary can leak malformed titles or media-type mismatches into every downstream surface, creating broken detail pages or bad AI resolution."}, {"id": "PRD-024", "severity": "important", "label": "Use timestamps for sync, sorting, and freshness", "why": "This matters because the plan covers timestamp conflict resolution but not explicit duplicate-item merge behavior, which is the edge case most likely to surface once sync is exercised across devices or runs."}, {"id": "PRD-078", "severity": "important", "label": "Codify the documented discovery pass bar", "why": "This matters because the plan proposes AI QA, but without the explicit pass thresholds the team could ship outputs that \"feel okay\" while still failing the benchmark’s stated quality bar."}, {"id": "PRD-051", "severity": "important", "label": "Use rich header media with graceful fallback", "why": "This matters because the detail page’s first-impression contract depends on motion-first media and premium sparse-media fallback; without that, the page can satisfy the checklist but still feel flat or cluttered."}, {"id": "PRD-052", "severity": "important", "label": "Show key facts and personal state immediately", "why": "This matters because the spec treats the first few seconds on Detail as orientation time; if rating/status are not intentionally surfaced early, users lose the fast save-and-decide workflow the page is supposed to enable."}]

top_gaps_json: [{"id": "PRD-034", "severity": "critical", "label": "Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades", "why": "The plan tracks schema versioning, but it never commits to automatic carry-forward of every saved show and every My Data field across upgrades. If this remains implicit, a migration could be technically “safe” while still failing the benchmark’s continuity promise."}, {"id": "PRD-005", "severity": "critical", "label": "Configure build through env without code edits", "why": "The plan includes env loading and examples, but it never states the benchmark’s stronger repo contract that setup must be entirely env-driven. That leaves a compliance gap around reproducible runs for fresh agents."}, {"id": "PRD-085", "severity": "important", "label": "Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces", "why": "The plan covers general AI quality and references voice indirectly, but it does not explicitly lock Scoop, Ask, Explore Similar, and Alchemy to a single shared persona. Without that, the product can ship with individually “good” AI surfaces that still feel like different products."}, {"id": "PRD-088", "severity": "important", "label": "Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review", "why": "The plan mentions a “Worth it?” style and voice adherence, but it omits most of the concrete Scoop section contract. top_gaps_json: [{"id": "PRD-069", "severity": "critical", "label": "Upgrades preserve libraries across model changes automatically", "why": "This matters because the plan includes schema versioning but not a concrete carry-forward strategy, so a future data-model migration could strand or corrupt saved libraries even if the initial build works."}, {"id": "PRD-053", "severity": "critical", "label": "Ask/Alchemy/Explore Similar use library, My Data, context", "why": "This matters because the product’s core promise is taste-aware discovery; if Alchemy and Explore Similar are only concept-driven and duplicate-aware, recommendations can feel generic instead of personalized."}, {"id": "PRD-065", "severity": "critical", "label": "Backend is source of truth; local cache disposable", "why": "This matters because the rider treats disposable local state as a hard execution constraint, and without an explicit plan for cache-clearing/reinstall safety, benchmark runs can pass locally while still violating durability expectations."}, {"id": "PRD-015", "severity": "critical", "label": "Per-field timestamps drive conflicts, freshness, sorting", "why": "This matters because merge correctness, recency ordering, and AI freshness all depend on timestamps; leaving sorting/use cases implicit makes data behavior drift likely under real usage or sync scenarios."}, {"id": "PRD-056", "severity": "important", "label": "Discovery quality demands specificity, surprise, real-show integrity", "why": "This matters because the AI features can be technically complete but still feel flat or off-brand if the plan never defines how to validate voice quality and “surprise without betrayal.”"}]

top_gaps_json: [{"id": "PRD-073", "severity": "critical", "label": "Stay spoiler-safe and inside TV/movie domain", "why": "Without this, Ask and Scoop can violate one of the product’s core trust boundaries by drifting into spoilers or non-entertainment chat instead of feeling safely opinionated."}, {"id": "PRD-026", "severity": "critical", "label": "Preserve synced data across conflicts and upgrades", "why": "The plan has some merge primitives, but it does not fully cover duplicate handling, settings continuity, or upgrade-safe carry-forward of user libraries, which risks silent data loss or divergence."}, {"id": "PRD-021", "severity": "critical", "label": "Clear status by deleting show and My Data", "why": "Removal semantics are only partially specified around reselecting Active, leaving ambiguous what happens when users clear other saved states and increasing the chance of orphaned or unexpectedly retained My Data."}, {"id": "PRD-001", "severity": "critical", "label": "Use Next.js/Supabase baseline without Docker dependency", "why": "The benchmark rider treats cloud-agent compatibility and Docker-optional execution as baseline compliance, so leaving this implicit creates avoidable execution risk even if the product plan is otherwise sound."}, {"id": "PRD-077", "severity": "critical", "label": "Treat real-show integrity as non-negotiable", "why": "The plan resolves AI output into catalog items, but it never treats hallucinated or mismatched titles as a hard fail condition, which is exactly the kind of regression the discovery quality bar is trying to prevent."}]
